_metadata.description: ARAH Cobra, Iron Grenadier, Cobra-La, and Oktober Guard characters — all named villains and generic troop types from the original 3.75" A Real American Hero toyline (1982-1994)

characters.name: Cobra Commander
characters.notes: Real name classified. Cobra supreme leader. Intelligence specialist. First released 1982 (mail-in), carded 1983. Multiple versions throughout the line

characters.name: Cobra Trooper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra infantry soldier. Also called 'Cobra Enemy' or 'Cobra Soldier' on packaging. First released 1982

characters.name: Cobra Officer
characters.notes: Generic Cobra field officer. First released 1982. Came with black AK-47 assault rifle

characters.name: Destro
characters.notes: Real name unknown on original filecard (later revealed as James McCullen Destro XXIV). Weapons manufacturer. Head of M.A.R.S. (Military Armaments Research System). Wears hereditary silver battle mask. First released 1983

characters.name: Major Bludd
characters.notes: Real name Sebastian Bludd. Born in Sydney, Australia. Mercenary and terrorist. Former Australian SAS and French Foreign Legion. First released 1983 (mail-in)

characters.name: H.I.S.S. Driver
characters.notes: Generic Cobra H.I.S.S. tank operator. Packaged exclusively with the Cobra H.I.S.S. Tank. First released 1983

characters.name: Viper Pilot
characters.notes: Generic Cobra F.A.N.G. gyrocopter pilot. Packaged exclusively with the Cobra F.A.N.G. First released 1983

characters.name: Baroness
characters.notes: Real name Anastasia DeCobray (birth name Cisarovna). Intelligence specialist and fixed-wing pilot. Spoiled offspring of wealthy European aristocrats. First released 1984

characters.name: Firefly
characters.notes: Real name classified (unknown even to Cobra Commander). Saboteur and demolitions expert. Expert in all NATO and Warsaw Pact explosives. First released 1984

characters.name: Scrap-Iron
characters.notes: Real name classified. Anti-armor specialist. Designs and field-tests all of Destro's weapons systems. First released 1984

characters.name: Storm Shadow
characters.notes: Real name Thomas S. Arashikage. Ninja and Cobra assassin. Member of the Arashikage clan. Sworn brother to Snake Eyes. Initially Cobra, later joins G.I. Joe. First released 1984

characters.name: Copperhead
characters.notes: Real name classified. Cobra Water Moccasin pilot. Swamp fighter and smuggler. Packaged with Water Moccasin vehicle. First released 1984

characters.name: Wild Weasel
characters.notes: Real name classified. Cobra Rattler pilot. Ace combat pilot who served in bush wars of South America and Africa. Packaged with Rattler. First released 1984

characters.name: Zartan
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Master of disguise and leader of the Dreadnoks. Can change skin color. Expert marksman, ventriloquist, and linguist. First released 1984

characters.name: Stinger Driver
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Stinger jeep operator. Packaged exclusively with the Cobra Night Attack Stinger 4-WD. First released 1984

characters.name: Buzzer
characters.notes: Real name Dick Blinken. Born in Ponsonby, Australia. Dreadnok. Former Cambridge sociology professor turned biker gang member. First released 1985

characters.name: Ripper
characters.notes: Real name Harry Nod. Born in Grim Cape, Tasmania. Dreadnok. Boatswain and smuggler. First released 1985

characters.name: Torch
characters.notes: Real name Tom Winken. Born in Botany Bay, Australia. Dreadnok. Cutting torch specialist. First released 1985

characters.name: Crimson Guard
characters.notes: Generic elite Cobra trooper known as 'Siegies'. Infiltrators posing as professionals in civilian life. First released 1985

characters.name: Eels
characters.notes: Generic Cobra frogman. Underwater demolitions specialist. First released 1985

characters.name: Snow Serpent
characters.notes: Generic Cobra arctic trooper. Snow operations specialist. First released 1985

characters.name: Tele-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra communications specialist. First released 1985

characters.name: Tomax
characters.notes: Real name Tomax Paoli. Crimson Guard Commander and twin brother of Xamot. Psychic bond with his twin. Extensive Enterprises CEO. First released 1985

characters.name: Xamot
characters.notes: Real name Xamot Paoli. Crimson Guard Commander and twin brother of Tomax. Distinguished by facial scar. Extensive Enterprises CEO. First released 1985

characters.name: Lamprey
characters.notes: Generic Cobra hydrofoil pilot. Packaged exclusively with the Cobra Moray Hydrofoil. First released 1985

characters.name: B.A.T.
characters.notes: Battle Android Trooper. Cobra's expendable robotic infantry. Distinctive exposed chest circuitry with interchangeable hand attachments. First released 1986

characters.name: Dr. Mindbender
characters.notes: Real name classified on original filecard (later identified as Brian Binder). Former orthodontist turned mad scientist. Creator of Serpentor. Master of mind control. First released 1986

characters.name: Monkeywrench
characters.notes: Real name Bill Winkie. Dreadnok. Explosives enthusiast who loves destroying things. First released 1986

characters.name: Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra infantry trooper. Replaced the original Cobra Trooper as the standard foot soldier. First released 1986

characters.name: Zandar
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Zartan's brother and Dreadnok. Master of camouflage and illusion. First released 1986

characters.name: Zarana
characters.notes: Real name Zoe (per Dreadnoks: Declassified). Zartan's sister and Dreadnok. Expert in disguise and undercover operations. First released 1986

characters.name: Serpentor
characters.notes: Genetically engineered Cobra Emperor. Created by Dr. Mindbender from DNA of history's greatest warriors. Packaged with Air Chariot. First released 1986

characters.name: A.V.A.C.
characters.notes: Air Viper Advanced Class. Generic Cobra Firebat pilot. Packaged with Cobra Terrordrome. First released 1986

characters.name: Motor-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra STUN driver. Packaged exclusively with the Cobra STUN vehicle. First released 1986

characters.name: Strato-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Night Raven pilot. Packaged exclusively with the Cobra Night Raven S3P. First released 1986

characters.name: Thrasher
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Dreadnok Thunder Machine driver. Youngest of the original Dreadnoks. Packaged with Thunder Machine. First released 1986

characters.name: Big Boa
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Cobra trainer and hand-to-hand combat instructor. Uses boxing as primary fighting style. First released 1987

characters.name: Croc Master
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Cobra reptile trainer who guards Cobra Island's perimeter with crocodiles. Self-trained alligator wrestler. First released 1987

characters.name: Crystal Ball
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Cobra hypnotist and interrogator. Claims to be a Romani mystic with the evil eye. Based on Vincent Price. First released 1987

characters.name: Raptor
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Cobra falconer and accountant. Uses trained birds of prey for reconnaissance. One of the more eccentric Cobra characters. First released 1987

characters.name: Techno-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra battlefield technician and combat engineer. Maintains Cobra's advanced weapons and vehicles. First released 1987

characters.name: Gyro-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Mamba helicopter pilot. Packaged with Cobra Mamba. First released 1987

characters.name: Ice Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Wolf arctic vehicle driver. Packaged with Cobra Wolf. First released 1987

characters.name: Sea Slug
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Sea Ray pilot. Packaged with Cobra Sea Ray. First released 1987

characters.name: Steam-Roller (Cobra)
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Maggot driver. Packaged with Cobra Maggot vehicle. First released 1987. Different character from the G.I. Joe Steam-Roller (Mobile Command Center driver). UNCERTAIN: sometimes listed as a named character vs generic trooper

characters.name: W.O.R.M.S.
characters.notes: Weapon Ordnance Rugged Machine Specialist. Generic Cobra Maggot gunner. Packaged with Cobra Maggot vehicle. First released 1987

characters.name: Zanzibar
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Dreadnok pirate and Air Skiff pilot. Packaged with Dreadnok Air Skiff. First released 1987

characters.name: Golobulus
characters.notes: Supreme ruler of Cobra-La. Ancient serpent-man with snake-like lower body. Main antagonist of G.I. Joe: The Movie (1987). Sold in Cobra-La 3-pack. First released 1987

characters.name: Nemesis Enforcer
characters.notes: Golobulus's winged enforcer and bodyguard. Bio-engineered creature, not fully human. Sold in Cobra-La 3-pack. First released 1987

characters.name: Royal Guard
characters.notes: Generic Cobra-La palace guard. Bred and surgically enhanced for size and strength to protect Golobulus. Sold in Cobra-La 3-pack. First released 1987

characters.name: Astro-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra space trooper. Pilots the Cobra Stellar Stiletto. First released 1988

characters.name: Hydro-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra advanced underwater operative. Underwater assault specialist. First released 1988

characters.name: Iron Grenadier
characters.notes: Generic elite trooper of Destro's personal military force. Gold and black livery. First released 1988

characters.name: Road Pig
characters.notes: Real name Donald DeLuca. Born in Goblu, Michigan. Dreadnok. Immensely strong brawler with a cinderblock hammer. Named after a Hasbro design director. First released 1988

characters.name: Toxo-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra hostile environment trooper. Handles toxic and chemical warfare materials. First released 1988

characters.name: Voltar
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Iron Grenadier general and Destro's field commander. First released 1988

characters.name: Nullifier
characters.notes: Generic Iron Grenadier A.G.P. (Anti-Gravity Pod) pilot. Packaged with the Iron Grenadier A.G.P. vehicle. First released 1988

characters.name: Secto-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra B.U.G.G. pilot. Packaged with the Cobra B.U.G.G. vehicle. First released 1988

characters.name: Star-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Stellar Stiletto pilot. Packaged with Cobra Stellar Stiletto. First released 1988. UNCERTAIN: may be same specialty as Astro-Viper (space operations)

characters.name: Alley Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra urban assault trooper. Specialists in city warfare. Distinctive orange and blue color scheme. First released 1989

characters.name: Frag-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra grenade thrower. Explosive ordnance specialist. First released 1989

characters.name: Gnawgahyde
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Dreadnok poacher and hunter. Accompanied by a wild boar named Clyde. First released 1989

characters.name: H.E.A.T. Viper
characters.notes: High Explosive Anti-Tank Viper. Generic Cobra bazooka trooper. Anti-armor specialist. First released 1989

characters.name: Night-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra night fighter. Specialist in nighttime operations and stealth. First released 1989

characters.name: Annihilator
characters.notes: Generic Iron Grenadier elite trooper. Destro's personal airborne assault force. First released 1989

characters.name: Darklon
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Destro's nephew and Iron Grenadier operative. Packaged with Darklon's Evader vehicle. First released 1989

characters.name: Aero-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Condor Z25 pilot. Packaged with Cobra Condor. First released 1989

characters.name: Track Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra H.I.S.S. II driver. Packaged with Cobra H.I.S.S. II vehicle. First released 1989

characters.name: Laser-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra laser trooper. Directed-energy weapons specialist. First released 1990

characters.name: Metal-Head
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Iron Grenadier anti-tank specialist. Destro's walking arsenal. First released 1990

characters.name: Night-Creeper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra techno-ninja mercenary. Blends high-tech gadgetry with traditional ninja skills. Hired by Cobra for espionage and assassination. First released 1990

characters.name: Range-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra wilderness trooper. Wilderness combat specialist. First released 1990

characters.name: Rock-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra mountain trooper. Alpine warfare specialist. First released 1990

characters.name: S.A.W.-Viper
characters.notes: Special Assault Weapons Viper. Generic Cobra heavy machine gunner. First released 1990

characters.name: Overlord
characters.notes: Real name Mikhail Derenko. Iron Grenadier commander. Packaged with Dictator vehicle. First released 1990

characters.name: Decimator
characters.notes: Generic Iron Grenadier Razorback driver. Packaged with Iron Grenadier Razorback vehicle. First released 1990. UNCERTAIN: may be a named character rather than generic

characters.name: Vapor
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Hammerhead pilot. Packaged with Cobra Hammerhead vehicle. First released 1990. UNCERTAIN: sometimes listed as named character vs generic trooper

characters.name: Crimson Guard Immortal
characters.notes: Generic elite Crimson Guard trooper. Advanced version of the original Crimson Guard. First released 1991

characters.name: Desert Scorpion
characters.notes: Generic Cobra desert trooper. Desert warfare specialist. First released 1991

characters.name: Incinerators
characters.notes: Generic Cobra flamethrower trooper. First released 1991

characters.name: Cesspool
characters.notes: Real name Vincent D'Alleva. Born in Newton, Massachusetts. Eco-Warriors Cobra operative. Former CEO of a chemical company disfigured in an industrial accident. Named after a Hasbro executive. First released 1991

characters.name: Sludge Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra toxic waste operative. Eco-Warriors sub-line. First released 1991

characters.name: Overkill
characters.notes: B.A.T. leader. Android commander of Cobra's Battle Android Troopers. Talking Battle Commanders sub-line. First released 1991 (Talking Battle Commanders), later 1992

characters.name: Red Star
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Oktober Guard team member. First Soviet character released in the ARAH line. First released 1991

characters.name: Dice
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Cobra Ninja Force operative. Partner to Slice. Specialist in pressure points, choke holds, and snares. Rumored former Night Creeper. First released 1992

characters.name: Slice
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Cobra Ninja Force swordsman. Renegade from the Arashikage clan. Cobra's supreme swordsman. Partner to Dice. First released 1992

characters.name: Flak-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra anti-aircraft trooper. First released 1992

characters.name: Headman
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Drug kingpin and leader of the Headhunters criminal organization. DEF (Drug Elimination Force) sub-line. Cobra-allied organized crime boss. First released 1992

characters.name: Headhunter
characters.notes: Generic Headman's drug enforcement thug. DEF (Drug Elimination Force) sub-line. First released 1992

characters.name: Toxo-Zombie
characters.notes: Cobra Eco-Warriors toxic waste mutant. Color-change gimmick reacting to water temperature. First released 1992

characters.name: Cobra Air Devil
characters.notes: Cobra Air Commandos operative. Paraglider-equipped air assault trooper. First released 1992

characters.name: Cobra Ninja Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra ninja trooper. Mail-in exclusive figure. First released 1992

characters.name: Big Bear
characters.notes: Oktober Guard team member. Only Oktober Guard character released as a carded figure during original ARAH run. First released 1992

characters.name: Night Creeper Leader
characters.notes: Real name unknown. Commander of the Night Creeper techno-ninja mercenary clan. Conducts all field and covert operations for Cobra. First released 1993

characters.name: Crimson Guard Commander
characters.notes: Generic elite officer of the Crimson Guard. Battle Corps sub-line. First released 1993

characters.name: Headhunter Stormtrooper
characters.notes: Generic elite Headhunter enforcer. Upgraded Headhunter trooper. First released 1993

characters.name: Bio-Viper
characters.notes: Genetically enhanced undersea monster created by Dr. Mindbender. Mega Marines sub-line. Not human. First released 1993

characters.name: Cyber-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra cyborg trooper. Mega Marines sub-line with moldable bio-armor. First released 1993

characters.name: Mega-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Mega-Monster trainer. Mega Marines sub-line with moldable bio-armor. First released 1993

characters.name: Monstro-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra bio-genetic Mega-Marine hunter. Mega Marines sub-line with moldable bio-armor. First released 1993

characters.name: Cobra B.A.A.T.
characters.notes: Battle Armored Android Trooper. Armored version of the B.A.T. for space operations. Star Brigade sub-line. First released 1993

characters.name: Cobra Blackstar
characters.notes: Cobra Star Brigade operative. Space warfare specialist. First released 1994

characters.name: Lobotomaxx
characters.notes: Alien character from the Lunartix Empire, allied with Cobra. Star Brigade sub-line. First released 1994

characters.name: Predacon
characters.notes: Alien character from the Lunartix Empire, allied with Cobra. Star Brigade sub-line. First released 1994

characters.name: Carcass
characters.notes: Alien character from the Lunartix Empire, allied with Cobra. Star Brigade sub-line. First released 1994

characters.name: Heli-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra helicopter assault trooper. Packaged with Cobra Liquidator vehicle. First released 1992

characters.name: Nitro-Viper
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Detonator driver. Packaged with Cobra Detonator vehicle. First released 1993

characters.name: Wild Boar
characters.notes: Generic Iron Grenadier Razorback gunner. Packaged with Iron Grenadier Razorback. First released 1989. UNCERTAIN: may overlap with Decimator as Razorback crew

characters.name: Ferret
characters.notes: Generic Iron Grenadier recon operative. Packaged with Iron Grenadier vehicle. First released 1988

characters.name: Ghostrider (Cobra)
characters.notes: Generic Cobra Phantom X-19 pilot. Packaged with Cobra Phantom X-19. First released 1988. Different character from the G.I. Joe Ghostrider (Phantom X-19 Stealth Fighter pilot)
